task_summary: End-to-end quarterly compliance pipeline with classify + flag + audit-log split + per-region summary
csv_content:
interaction_id,date,region,channel,customer_segment,subject,description,resolved,risk_score
INT-2001,2026-07-01,US-NE,phone,retail,Account access locked after travel,Customer reported account lockout following international travel; requested manual unlock following our standard verification protocol.,yes,2
INT-2002,2026-07-02,US-NE,email,retail,Disputed transaction,Customer disputes a $342 transaction at an online merchant; suspects unauthorized use; wants chargeback initiated.,no,4
INT-2003,2026-07-03,US-SW,phone,smb,Wire transfer hold question,Small business customer asking why outgoing wire to international vendor was held for additional review; concerned about supplier payment deadlines.,yes,3
INT-2004,2026-07-04,US-SW,email,retail,Statement clarification,Customer asks for clarification of a fee labelled 'maintenance adjustment' on monthly statement; requests fee waiver.,yes,1
INT-2005,2026-07-05,US-NE,phone,wealth,Unauthorized access concern,Wealth management client reports receiving a security alert email they did not initiate; concerned about identity compromise.,no,5
INT-2006,2026-07-06,EU-W,email,retail,Refund request,Customer requesting refund of overdraft fee following misunderstanding about pending deposit timing.,yes,1
INT-2007,2026-07-07,EU-W,phone,smb,Loan modification ask,Small business asking about hardship modification options on outstanding term loan; cited reduced revenue.,no,2
INT-2008,2026-07-08,APAC,email,wealth,Address change request,Wealth client requesting change of mailing address following relocation; requested confirmation of process.,yes,1
INT-2009,2026-07-09,APAC,phone,retail,Suspected phishing,Customer reports receiving an email impersonating the bank asking them to verify SSN and account details; did not click.,no,4
INT-2010,2026-07-10,US-NE,email,smb,Bulk wire reversal request,Small business requested reversal of three outgoing wires totalling $48,000 due to duplicate payment to vendor.,no,3
INT-2011,2026-07-11,US-SW,phone,retail,Identity theft report,Customer reports two new accounts opened in their name without authorization; requesting all accounts frozen and a full credit-bureau review initiated.,no,5
INT-2012,2026-07-12,EU-W,email,wealth,Trust documentation request,Wealth client asking for current trust documentation package for their estate planning attorney; needs notarised copies.,yes,1
INT-2013,2026-07-13,APAC,phone,retail,ATM card swallowed,Customer ATM card swallowed by machine in different country during travel; requesting emergency replacement.,yes,2
INT-2014,2026-07-14,US-NE,email,retail,Disputed recurring charge,Customer disputing a recurring $14.99 subscription charge; says they cancelled the service in March but charges continued.,no,2
INT-2015,2026-07-15,US-SW,phone,smb,Loan covenant question,Small business asking whether reduced quarterly revenue triggers a financial covenant breach on their term loan; requesting written guidance.,no,3
opening_prompt: Hi — for the upcoming quarterly review I need a more comprehensive workflow than what we've done before. The uploaded file is compliance_q3_full.csv with this period's interactions, including a region field this time. What we need, depending on how the data sorts: (1) each entry classified into the second-line review categories as before — Account Access, Transaction Dispute, Fee Inquiry, Security Concern, Lending/Modification, Operational/Other; (2) anything touching unauthorised access, identity matters, suspected phishing, or a risk_score of 4 or higher needs to be split out for our fraud team — those have separate evidentiary handling; (3) the regular categorisation file should additionally be broken down by region — US-NE, US-SW, EU-W, APAC — because each region's compliance lead needs only their own entries; (4) we'd want a separate small summary file showing per-region counts of each category and how many were flagged for fraud, for our second-line review meeting. I realise that's quite a few moving parts; could we set this up such that the regional leads each receive only their region's regular-category file and the fraud team receives all the flagged ones in one consolidated file?
probe: Multi-step pipeline (classify -> dual-condition fork -> per-region split -> aggregate-and-summary sink) phrased through hedges. Tests: (1) does the composer decompose the four asks into pipeline stages, (2) does it correctly express the OR-condition for fraud routing (categories OR risk_score >= 4), (3) does it produce N+1 sinks (per-region categorized + consolidated fraud + summary) without losing track of token routing, (4) does it converge in <=3 user turns despite the verbosity.
product_capability_used: csv source + LLM classify transform + value_transform (derive flag from category OR risk_score) + gate routing on flag + per-region splitter (gate-on-region or fork-on-region) + aggregation/summary transform + multiple JSONL/CSV sinks
expected_outcome: Either: (a) full pipeline producing 4 region files + 1 fraud file + 1 summary file (high bar); (b) full pipeline minus the per-region split, producing 1 categorized file + 1 fraud file + 1 summary, with explicit acknowledgement that per-region split needs a separate run (acceptable); (c) composer convergence-timeout (failure mode worth surfacing).
pass_criterion: Real on-disk output AND the fraud-flag set is non-empty AND the summary file's per-region counts sum to total rows. Bonus signal: did the composer name the OR-condition explicitly, or did it silently drop the risk_score arm?
